nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: Hehe, another youngster has come to Peach Blossom Island. Did you come for this old man's reputation, or did you stumble here by accident?

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: I am Huang Yaoshi, known as the Eastern Heretic. Proficient in the Eight Trigrams, music, Go, calligraphy, and painting. I've heard you meet friends through Go - interesting.

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: This old man's 'Eight Trigram Arts' are mastered thoroughly, and when applied to Go, the variations are endless. However, you must first prove you're qualified to learn.

options.text: Please teach me, Island Master Huang!
I want to learn the 'Eight Trigram Arts'
Island Master Huang, I'd like to play another game with you
How are the Eight Trigrams used in Go?

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: The Eight Trigrams emphasize 'unpredictable changes, winning through surprise.' On the Go board, you can set up mysterious formations that trap opponents in a maze.

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: This technique allows you to change the game's pattern at crucial moments, creating unexpected variations. However, it requires extremely high insight to master.

options.text: Exquisite! Please teach me, Island Master Huang

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: Good! You have courage! This old man hates pedantic people most - your boldness suits my taste.

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: Years ago at Mount Hua, this old man played against Hong Qigong and Ouyang Feng - each had their merits. If you can beat this old man on the Go board, I'll acknowledge your real skill.

options.text: Please bestow your teaching, Island Master Huang!

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: Haha, then come! Let you witness the power of the Eastern Heretic!

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: Excellent, excellent! The younger generation is formidable! Your game is unbounded and imaginative, quite like this old man in his prime. Good, I'll teach you the 'Eight Trigram Arts.'

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: The key to this technique is: use the principles of the Eight Trigrams to set up mysterious formations on the Go board, trapping your opponent in difficulty. Can be used once per game with a 35-move cooldown.

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: Remember, 'troops have no constant formation, water has no constant shape.' When using this technique, be flexible and don't be constrained by form. This old man has lived unconventionally - you must also learn to break conventions.

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: By the way, my daughter Rong'er is in Xiangyang. When you visit her, give her my regards. Her 'Mechanism Mastery' was also taught by this old man.

options.text: I'd like to hear more of your insights
I want to challenge you again

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: Your Go skill still needs refinement. But not bad - practice more and don't disappoint this old man.

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: This old man has lived unconventionally, seeking only freedom and ease. Worldly conventions are all shackles to this old man. The same goes for Go - you must break through preconceptions.

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: However, though this old man is the 'Eastern Heretic,' I never do unconscionable things. Go reflects character - remember this.

options.text: Island Master Huang is noble and upright!

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: Very well, come back when you're ready. Peach Blossom Island is here, and this old man will be waiting anytime.

nodes.speaker: Huang Yaoshi
nodes.text: Haha, youngster, work hard! Looking forward to your achievements on the path of Go in the future!
